channel_title: AI News & Strategy Daily | Nate B Jones
title: Stripe, Visa, Mastercard, Microsoft, Meta. All Building The Same Thing.
executive_summary: Stripe has introduced a suite of new agent commerce products, marking a significant shift in the internet economy where power is transferring from sellers to buyers for the first time in decades. This transition is crucial for anyone involved in online economic activities, as it represents the largest change in commerce patterns in 20 years. The focus is on how AI agents will facilitate purchases and the implications for businesses.
bullets: Stripe announced a new suite of agent commerce products, which signifies a major shift in the internet economy where 'the power is shifting from the seller to the buyer for the first time in decades.' This shift is like moving from a world where shopkeepers control the store to one where customers dictate what they want without stepping inside.

The old sales funnel was designed to make human intent visible, with stages like awareness, consideration, and conversion. Think of it like a funnel that collects rainwater: it captures every drop (customer behavior) as it flows down, allowing sellers to optimize their strategies based on what they see.

Payment authority is now moving with the task rather than being confined to the checkout process. Imagine carrying a pre-loaded gift card that already has a specific amount for a specific store; you don’t need to decide how to pay at the moment of purchase because the payment method is already set.

Stripe's new Links wallet allows agents to request payments programmatically, requiring user approval before sharing payment credentials. This is like giving your friend a prepaid card to buy you lunch, but they must check with you first before making the purchase.

For an AI agent to make a purchase on your behalf, it must understand your preferences, the seller's details, and the transaction constraints. It's like teaching a child how to shop: they need to know what you like, what the store offers, and how much money they can spend.

The commercial landscape is evolving from a seller-controlled environment to one where buyers' agents dictate the purchasing process. This is akin to a movie where the director (the agent) now decides the scenes (purchases) without the actors (sellers) controlling the script.

Stripe's agent commerce suite includes features like shared payment tokens and machine payments protocols, which are designed to facilitate transactions before the seller even interacts with the buyer. Think of it as setting up a direct line for a delivery service to drop off groceries without needing to place an order each time.

The shift to agent commerce means that businesses must provide structured information about their products, including prices and policies, in a way that agents can easily understand. It's like creating a user manual for a complex machine: if the instructions aren't clear, the machine won't operate correctly.

Google is transitioning from being a search engine to a platform that supports AI-driven commerce, moving beyond traditional keyword searches. This is like a librarian who now not only finds books but also curates personalized reading lists based on your interests.

Stripe's Radar system is designed to combat fraud in an agent-driven economy, where automated agents could exploit vulnerabilities. Imagine a security guard who not only checks IDs but also monitors for suspicious behavior in real-time, ensuring safety for everyone.

The agentic economy will require businesses to adapt their marketing strategies, focusing on being usable by agents rather than just appealing to human customers. This is like a restaurant that needs to ensure its menu is clear and accessible not just to diners but also to delivery robots.

The introduction of streaming payments and usage-based billing will allow for more flexible and efficient transactions in the agent economy. It's like a subscription service that charges you based on how much you use it, rather than a flat fee, making it fairer for both the provider and the consumer.
key_takeaways: Implement a strategy to ensure your product information is structured and easily readable by AI agents. This is crucial as agents will be making purchasing decisions on behalf of users, and if your information isn't clear, you risk losing sales. Without this, your business may become invisible in the new agent-driven economy.

By the end of the year, ensure that your payment systems can accommodate agent-driven transactions, including shared payment tokens. This is vital because as agents become more prevalent, businesses that can't adapt will be left behind. If you don’t act, you may miss out on significant market opportunities.

Set a rule that at least 50% of your product evaluations must focus on agent usability, not just human appeal. This shift is essential because agents will increasingly dictate purchasing decisions, and failing to adapt could lead to decreased sales and relevance in the market.

technical_terms.Agent Commerce: A new model of commerce where AI agents facilitate purchases on behalf of users, shifting control from sellers to buyers. Think of it like a personal shopper who knows your preferences and makes purchases for you without needing your constant input.
technical_terms.Payment Authority: The ability for a buyer to control how and when payments are made, now shifting to be associated with specific tasks rather than just the checkout process. Like having a prepaid card that allows you to buy specific items without needing to decide how to pay each time.
technical_terms.Shared Payment Tokens: A secure method that allows agents to make purchases without exposing the buyer's actual payment details. Think of it like a one-time-use gift card that can only be used for a specific transaction.
technical_terms.Streaming Payments: A payment model that allows for continuous transactions based on usage rather than a single lump sum. Like a water bill that charges you based on how much water you actually use each month, rather than a flat fee.
technical_terms.Radar: Stripe's fraud detection system designed to protect against fraudulent transactions in an agent-driven economy. Imagine a security system that learns from past incidents to prevent future thefts.
technical_terms.Machine Payments Protocol: A set of standards that facilitate transactions between machines and services without human intervention. Think of it like a highway designed specifically for delivery trucks, allowing them to move efficiently without stopping for traffic.
technical_terms.Links Wallet: A feature that allows users to give agents programmatic access to their payment methods for easier transactions. Like a digital key that lets your friend borrow your car without needing to hand over the keys each time.
technical_terms.Usage-Based Billing: A billing model that charges customers based on their actual usage of a service rather than a flat rate. Like a gym that charges you only for the days you actually work out, making it fairer for occasional users.
technical_terms.Agentic Visibility: The concept of making product information clear and accessible for AI agents to facilitate purchasing decisions. Think of it like having a well-organized library where every book is labeled and easy to find.
technical_terms.Commercial Stack: The collection of tools and services that businesses use to operate in the agentic economy. Like a toolbox that contains all the essential tools needed to build and maintain a house.
